title: task
description:
rust/lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs:177 - The condition 'version_is_newer(dio.version, self.version) || !self.is_joined()' allows a node that lost all parents (became Unjoined) to accept a DIO with an older DODAG version. This violates RFC 6550 Section 8.2.2.6 which states 'A node MUST NOT rejoin an older version of a DODAG'.

Failure scenario:
1. Node joins DODAG version 5
2. All parents fail, node becomes Unjoined (but version=5 is retained)
3. Stale DIO arrives with version=3
4. version_is_newer(3,5)=false, but !self.is_joined()=true
5. Node adopts version 3, regressing to stale network state

Fix: Remove '|| !self.is_joined()' from the condition. A fresh node with version=0 will still join because version_is_newer(X,0)=true for any X>0 in the linear region, or the versions will be equal and the node will proceed to add the parent normally.
close_reason: Fixed: Removed || !self.is_joined() condition that allowed unjoined nodes to adopt older DODAG versions, preventing version downgrade attacks
